metadata.description: 综合评估问题集 - PDF(50) + HTML(30) + CSV(9)

questions.question: What does BERT stand for?
questions.answer: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers

questions.question: What pre-training objectives does BERT use?
questions.answer: Masked Language Model (MLM) and Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)

questions.question: What score did BERT achieve on SQuAD v1.1 Test F1?
What is the main advantage of BERT over left-to-right language models?
questions.answer: BERT uses masked language models to enable pretrained deep bidirectional representations, jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers

questions.question: What is the GLUE score achieved by BERT?
What is RAG in the context of NLP?
questions.answer: Retrieval-Augmented Generation - a model that combines pre-trained parametric and non-parametric memory for language generation

questions.question: What are the two RAG model formulations proposed in the paper?
questions.answer: RAG-Sequence (uses the same retrieved document to generate the complete sequence) and RAG-Token (can draw a different latent document for each target token)

questions.question: What retrieval component does RAG use?
questions.answer: DPR (Dense Passage Retriever), which follows a bi-encoder architecture

questions.question: What is the parametric memory in RAG?
questions.answer: A pre-trained seq2seq model (BART)

questions.question: What is the non-parametric memory in RAG?
questions.answer: A dense vector index of Wikipedia, accessed with a pre-trained neural retriever

questions.question: What is the main contribution of the Transformer paper?
questions.answer: A new simple network architecture based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely

questions.question: What BLEU score did the Transformer achieve on WMT 2014 English-to-German translation?
questions.answer: 28.4 BLEU

questions.question: What is the key mechanism in the Transformer architecture?
questions.answer: Self-attention mechanism (scaled dot-product attention, multi-head attention)

questions.question: What problem does the Transformer solve compared to RNNs?
questions.answer: The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization and overcomes the fundamental constraint of sequential computation in RNNs

questions.question: What are the main components of the Transformer architecture?
questions.answer: Encoder and decoder, both using self-attention mechanisms and feed-forward networks

questions.question: What is GPT-4?
questions.answer: A large-scale, multimodal model which can accept image and text inputs and produce text outputs

questions.question: How did GPT-4 perform on the simulated bar exam?
questions.answer: GPT-4 achieves a score that falls in the top 10% of test takers

questions.question: What training approach is used for GPT-4?
questions.answer: Pre-trained to predict the next token in a document, then fine-tuned using Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)

questions.question: What is the MMLU benchmark?
questions.answer: An English-language suite of multiple-choice questions covering 57 subjects

questions.question: What is one of GPT-4's main limitations?
questions.answer: It is not fully reliable (can suffer from 'hallucinations'), has a limited context window, and does not learn from experience

questions.question: What is Llama 2?
questions.answer: A collection of pretrained and fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) ranging in scale from 7 billion to 70 billion parameters

questions.question: What is Llama 2-Chat optimized for?
questions.answer: Dialogue use cases

questions.question: What fine-tuning techniques does Llama 2 use?
questions.answer: Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF)

questions.question: Who released Llama 2?
questions.answer: Meta (GenAI, Meta)

questions.question: What are the parameter sizes available for Llama 2?
questions.answer: 7 billion to 70 billion parameters

questions.question: What is LoRA?
questions.answer: Low-Rank Adaptation - a method that freezes pretrained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture

questions.question: What are the advantages of LoRA over full fine-tuning?
questions.answer: LoRA can reduce the number of trainable parameters by 10,000 times and the GPU memory requirement by 3 times, with no additional inference latency

questions.question: What is the key hypothesis behind LoRA?
questions.answer: The change in weights during model adaptation has a low 'intrinsic rank'

questions.question: Which models were tested with LoRA?
questions.answer: RoBERTa, DeBERTa, GPT-2, and GPT-3

questions.question: What is the main problem with full fine-tuning for large models like GPT-3 175B?
questions.answer: Deploying independent instances of fine-tuned models, each with 175B parameters, is prohibitively expensive

questions.question: What is Ragas?
questions.answer: A framework for reference-free evaluation of Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines

questions.question: What are the three main metrics proposed in Ragas?
questions.answer: Faithfulness, Answer Relevance, and Context Relevance

questions.question: What does Faithfulness measure in Ragas?
questions.answer: Whether the answer can be inferred from the given context (grounded in the context)

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Ragas over traditional evaluation methods?
questions.answer: Ragas can evaluate RAG systems without relying on ground truth human annotations

questions.question: What are the two main limitations of using LLMs as knowledge bases?
questions.answer: LLMs are not able to answer questions about events after their training, and struggle to memorize knowledge that is rarely mentioned in the training corpus

questions.question: What approach does RAG use to generate answers?
questions.answer: RAG retrieves top-K documents using DPR, then marginalizes over these documents when generating output using BART

questions.question: What is the purpose of the position representation in the Transformer?
questions.answer: Since the Transformer contains no recurrence and no convolution, position encodings are used to inject information about the relative or absolute position of tokens

questions.question: What is Multi-Head Attention in the Transformer?
questions.answer: Instead of performing a single attention function, queries, keys and values are linearly projected h times with different learned linear projections, and attention is performed in parallel

questions.question: What is the difference between GPT-4 and GPT-3.5 on the bar exam?
questions.answer: GPT-4 scores in the top 10% while GPT-3.5 scores in the bottom 10%

questions.question: How does Llama 2-Chat compare to other chat models?
questions.answer: Our models outperform open-source chat models on most benchmarks tested, and may be a suitable substitute for closed-source models

questions.question: What problem do RAG models solve that pure language models cannot?
questions.answer: RAG models can access and precisely manipulate knowledge from external sources, can easily expand or revise memory, and reduce hallucinations

questions.question: What is Scaled Dot-Product Attention?
questions.answer: Attention(Q,K,V) = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k))V, where Q is queries, K is keys, V is values, and d_k is the dimension of keys

questions.question: What are the two main strategies for applying pre-trained language representations to downstream tasks?
questions.answer: Feature-based (using pre-trained representations as additional features) and Fine-tuning (updating all pretrained parameters)

questions.question: What is the 'next sentence prediction' task in BERT?
questions.answer: A task that jointly pretrains text-pair representations by predicting whether one sentence follows another

questions.question: What dataset does RAG use as its non-parametric memory?
questions.answer: Wikipedia (December 2018 dump)

questions.question: How many documents does RAG typically retrieve?
questions.answer: The paper considers K ∈ {5, 10} for training and sets K for test time using dev data

questions.question: What is the role of LoRA's rank r parameter?
questions.answer: r controls the rank of the low-rank decomposition matrices; a very low rank (1 or 2) suffices even when the full rank is as high as 12,288

questions.question: What is the main challenge in evaluating RAG systems according to Ragas?
questions.answer: There are several dimensions to consider: retrieval quality, LLM's ability to use passages faithfully, and generation quality

questions.question: What is the BLEU score of the Transformer on WMT 2014 English-to-French translation?
questions.answer: 41.8 BLEU

questions.question: What are the three main approaches to task decomposition mentioned for LLM agents?
questions.answer: 1) LLM with simple prompting, 2) Task-specific instructions, 3) Human inputs

questions.question: What is LangChain?
questions.answer: A framework for building applications with LLMs, providing standard interfaces for chat models, embeddings, tools, and agents across multiple providers

questions.question: What features do LangChain chat models support besides text generation?
questions.answer: Tool calling, structured output, multimodality (images, audio, video), and reasoning

questions.question: How can you initialize a chat model in LangChain?
questions.answer: Using init_chat_model() from a chat model provider like OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, AWS Bedrock, HuggingFace, etc.

questions.question: What is RAG in the context of LangChain?
questions.answer: Retrieval-Augmented Generation - a pattern that retrieves relevant documents and passes them as context to an LLM to generate answers grounded in specific data

questions.question: What are the key components of a RAG chain in LangChain?
questions.answer: Indexing content (loading, splitting, storing/embedding), RAG chain (retrieve, augment, generate), and RAG agent

questions.question: What is an LLM-powered autonomous agent?
questions.answer: An AI system where LLM functions as the agent's brain, complemented by planning, memory, and tool use components

questions.question: What are the three key components of an LLM agent system?
questions.answer: Planning (subgoal decomposition, reflection), Memory (short-term in-context learning, long-term external vector store), and Tool use (calling external APIs)

questions.question: What is Chain of Thought (CoT) prompting?
questions.answer: A technique where the model is instructed to 'think step by step' to utilize more test-time computation to decompose hard tasks into smaller steps

questions.question: What is ReAct in the context of LLM agents?
questions.answer: A framework that integrates reasoning and acting within LLM by extending the action space to combine task-specific actions with language space for reasoning traces

questions.question: What is prompt engineering?
questions.answer: Methods for how to communicate with LLM to steer its behavior for desired outcomes without updating the model weights

questions.question: What is zero-shot learning in prompt engineering?
questions.answer: Simply feeding the task text to the model and asking for results without providing any examples

questions.question: What is few-shot learning in prompt engineering?
questions.answer: Presenting a set of high-quality demonstrations, each consisting of both input and desired output, to help the model understand the task

questions.question: What three biases affect few-shot classification performance?
questions.answer: Majority label bias (unbalanced label distribution), Recency bias (model repeats the last label), and Common token bias (LLM produces common tokens more often)

questions.question: What is Tree of Thoughts (ToT)?
questions.answer: A method that extends CoT by exploring multiple reasoning possibilities at each step, creating a tree structure with BFS or DFS search

questions.question: How does Reflexion improve LLM agents?
questions.answer: It is a framework that equips agents with dynamic memory and self-reflection capabilities to improve reasoning skills by learning from past mistakes

questions.question: What is LlamaIndex?
questions.answer: A data framework for building LLM applications, providing tools for data ingestion, indexing, and querying over private data

questions.question: How does RAG work in LlamaIndex?
questions.answer: LLMs are trained on enormous bodies of data but not on your data. RAG solves this by adding your data to the data LLMs can access when answering questions

questions.question: What is the AnswerRelevancyEvaluator in LlamaIndex?
questions.answer: An evaluator that assesses the relevancy of a response to a query, considering both the query string and response string

questions.question: What data connectors does LlamaIndex support?
questions.answer: Connectors for loading data from various sources including databases, APIs, file systems, and third-party services

questions.question: What is a query engine in LlamaIndex?
questions.answer: An interface for asking questions over your data, supporting different retrieval strategies and response synthesis methods

questions.question: What is the Transformer architecture?
questions.answer: A model architecture based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely

questions.question: What is multi-head attention in Transformers?
questions.answer: Instead of performing a single attention function, queries, keys and values are linearly projected h times with different learned projections, and attention is performed in parallel

questions.question: What is the key advantage of Transformer over RNNs?
questions.answer: The Transformer allows for significantly more parallelization and requires significantly less time to train, as it removes the sequential computation constraint of RNNs

questions.question: What is Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE)?
questions.answer: A position encoding method that encodes absolute position with a rotation matrix and naturally incorporates relative position information in self-attention

questions.question: How does the RAG-Token model differ from RAG-Sequence?
questions.answer: RAG-Token can retrieve different documents for each token during generation, while RAG-Sequence uses the same document for all tokens

questions.question: What is the purpose of a vector database in RAG?
questions.answer: To store document embeddings and enable efficient similarity search for retrieving relevant context documents based on query semantics

questions.question: What is the LlamaIndex agent used for?
questions.answer: An agent that can use tools and reason about how to answer complex queries, combining LLM reasoning with tool execution capabilities

questions.question: What is the role of response synthesis in RAG systems?
questions.answer: The process of generating a final answer by combining retrieved context with the user query using an LLM

questions.question: What techniques can be used to improve a RAG chatbot's performance?
questions.answer: Techniques include prompt engineering, proper chunking strategies, metadata filtering, reranking, and using appropriate embedding models

questions.question: What are the advantages of using LangChain's standard model interface?
questions.answer: It allows swapping providers without rewriting application logic, supports multiple providers through dedicated integration packages with the same API

questions.question: What chunk strategy is best for production-level systems?
questions.answer: Parent document chunking (small chunks for retrieval, large chunks for context) with child=256 and parent=1024

questions.question: Which chunk strategy retains hierarchical structure?
questions.answer: Markdown Header chunking, which splits by Markdown headings and preserves the document hierarchy

questions.question: What is the recommended chunk strategy for general use?
questions.answer: Recursive character chunking, which splits by separator levels balancing speed and quality

questions.question: What is the best embedding model for Chinese language tasks?
questions.answer: BAAI/bge-large-zh-v1.5 with 1024 dimensions, Chinese MTEB score of 64.2, open source

questions.question: Which embedding model has the highest English MTEB score?
questions.answer: BAAI/bge-m3 with English MTEB score of 65.3, followed by text-embedding-3-large with 64.6

questions.question: Which embedding model supports multilingual scenarios?
questions.answer: BAAI/bge-m3 with 1024 dimensions, best for multilingual use cases

questions.question: How does DeepSeek-R1 compare to GPT-4 on MMLU?
questions.answer: DeepSeek-R1 achieves 90.8 on MMLU, outperforming GPT-4's 86.4, with 671B parameters and open-source

questions.question: Which open-source model has the best coding performance?
questions.answer: DeepSeek-R1 with 80.0 Coding score, followed by DeepSeek-V3 with 75.3 and GPT-4o with 73.6

questions.question: What is the best performing model on Math benchmarks?
questions.answer: DeepSeek-R1 with 79.8 on Math, followed by Claude 3.5 Sonnet with 71.1
